{{神社| 名称=十日恵比須神社| 所在地=福岡県福岡市博多区東公園7-1| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|36|14|N|130|25|07|E|}}| 祭神=事代主命
大国主命| 創建=天正19年（1591年）| 社格=別表神社| 例祭=[[1月8日]] - [[1月10日|10日]]（十日恵比須祭）}} '''十日恵比須神社'''（とおかえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[博多区]]にある[[神社]]である。 [[事代主|事代主命]]・[[大国主|大国主命]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[香椎宮]]大宮司家の武内五右衛門は分家して「神屋」と号して博多で商売を営んでいたが、[[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）1月3日、香椎宮・[[筥崎宮]]を参拝したとき、香椎浜に流れついた恵比須神の[[神像]]を得た。それを自宅に持ち帰って祀ったところ、家運が隆盛した。そのため多くの人が商売繁昌の神として参拝するようになったので、[[元禄]]3年（[[1690年]]）1月10日に社殿を設けた。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www7.ocn.ne.jp/~t-ebisu/ 十日恵比須神社]（公式サイト） [[Category:福岡県の神社|とおかえひすしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|とおかえひすしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=十日恵比須神社| 所在地=福岡県福岡市博多区[[東公園 (福岡市)|東公園]]7-1| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|36|14|N|130|25|07|E|}}| 祭神=事代主命
大国主命| 創建=天正19年（1591年）| 社格=別表神社| 例祭=[[1月8日]] - [[1月10日|10日]]（十日恵比須祭）}} '''十日恵比須神社'''（とおかえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[博多区]]にある[[神社]]である。 [[事代主|事代主命]]・[[大国主|大国主命]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[香椎宮]]大宮司家の武内五右衛門は分家して「神屋」と号して博多で商売を営んでいたが、[[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）1月3日、香椎宮・[[筥崎宮]]を参拝したとき、香椎浜に流れついた恵比須神の[[神像]]を得た。それを自宅に持ち帰って祀ったところ、家運が隆盛した。そのため多くの人が商売繁昌の神として参拝するようになったので、[[元禄]]3年（[[1690年]]）1月10日に社殿を設けた。 ==交通== *[[福岡市地下鉄箱崎線]] [[千代県庁口駅]] *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[鹿児島本線]] [[吉塚駅]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www7.ocn.ne.jp/~t-ebisu/ 十日恵比須神社]（公式サイト） {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえひすしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:えびす神社]] [[Category:博多区]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=十日恵比須神社| 所在地=福岡県福岡市博多区[[東公園 (福岡市)|東公園]]7-1| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|36|14|N|130|25|07|E|}}| 祭神=事代主命
大国主命| 創建=天正19年（1591年）| 社格=別表神社| 例祭=[[1月8日]] - [[1月11日|11日]]（十日恵比須祭）}} '''十日恵比須神社'''（とおかえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[博多区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[事代主|事代主命]]（恵比須神）・[[大国主|大国主命]]（大黒神）を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[香椎宮]]大宮司家の武内五右衛門（平十郎）は分家して「神屋」と号して博多で商売を営んでいたが、[[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）1月3日、香椎宮・[[筥崎宮]]を参拝したとき、香椎浜に流れついた恵比須神の[[神像]]2体を得た。それを自宅に持ち帰って祀ったところ家運が隆盛。翌年の[[文禄]]元年（[[1592年]]）1月10日、平十郎が神像を拾い上げた場所に社殿を設けた。そののち[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[1681年]]）11月15日、四代目平十郎が創建90年にあたって千代の[[崇福寺 (福岡市)|崇福寺]]境内に社殿を再建する。 [[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]）10月、福岡県の指示により崇福寺境内から[[東公園 (福岡市)|東公園]]に遷座する。[[昭和]]4年（[[1929年]]）には東公園整備に伴って現在の地に遷座する。昭和43年（[[1968年]]）には[[別表神社]]に加わる。 ==十日恵比須正月大祭== 毎年[[1月8日]]から[[1月11日]]まで正月大祭が執り行われる。8日が「初えびす」、9日が「宵えびす」、10日が「正大祭」、11日が「残りえびす」と呼ばれる。正月大祭期間中は ==交通== *[[福岡市地下鉄箱崎線]] [[千代県庁口駅]] *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[鹿児島本線]] [[吉塚駅]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www7.ocn.ne.jp/~t-ebisu/ 十日恵比須神社]（公式サイト） {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえひすしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:えびす神社]] [[Category:博多区]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=十日恵比須神社| 所在地=福岡県福岡市博多区[[東公園 (福岡市)|東公園]]7-1| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|36|14|N|130|25|07|E|}}| 祭神=事代主命
大国主命| 創建=天正19年（1591年）| 社格=別表神社| 例祭=[[1月8日]] - [[1月11日|11日]]（十日恵比須祭）}} '''十日恵比須神社'''（とおかえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[博多区]]にある[[神社]]である。[[事代主|事代主命]]（恵比須神）・[[大国主|大国主命]]（大黒神）を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[香椎宮]]大宮司家の武内五右衛門（平十郎）は分家して「神屋」と号して博多で商売を営んでいたが、[[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）1月3日、香椎宮・[[筥崎宮]]を参拝したとき、香椎浜に流れついた恵比須神の[[神像]]2体を得た。それを自宅に持ち帰って祀ったところ家運が隆盛。翌年の[[文禄]]元年（[[1592年]]）1月10日、平十郎が神像を拾い上げた場所に社殿を設けた。そののち[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[1681年]]）11月15日、四代目平十郎が創建90年にあたって千代の[[崇福寺 (福岡市)|崇福寺]]境内に社殿を再建する。 [[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]）10月、福岡県の指示により崇福寺境内から[[東公園 (福岡市)|東公園]]に遷座する。[[昭和]]4年（[[1929年]]）には東公園整備に伴って現在の地に遷座する。昭和43年（[[1968年]]）には[[別表神社]]に加わる。 ==十日恵比須正月大祭== 毎年[[1月8日]]から[[1月11日]]まで正月大祭が執り行われる。8日が「初えびす」、9日が「宵えびす」、10日が「正大祭」、11日が「残りえびす」と呼ばれる。正月大祭期間中は福引きや[[芸妓]]かち詣りなどが有名。 ==交通== *[[福岡市地下鉄箱崎線]] [[千代県庁口駅]] *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[鹿児島本線]] [[吉塚駅]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www7.ocn.ne.jp/~t-ebisu/ 十日恵比須神社]（公式サイト） {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえひすしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:えびす神社]] [[Category:博多区]] {{shinto-stub}}

大国主命 | 創建=天正19年（1591年） | 社格=別表神社 | 例祭=[[1月8日]] - [[1月11日|11日]]（十日恵比須祭） | 地図=Fukuoka city }} '''十日恵比須神社'''（とおかえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[博多区]]にある[[神社]]{{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web | author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}である。[[事代主|事代主命]]（恵比須神）・[[大国主|大国主命]]（大黒神）を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html 十日恵比寿について]十日恵比寿神社、[[香椎宮]]大宮司家の武内五右衛門（平十郎）は分家して「神屋」と号して博多で商売を営んでいたが、[[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）1月3日、香椎宮・[[筥崎宮]]を参拝したとき、香椎浜に流れついた恵比須神の[[神像]]2体を得た。それを自宅に持ち帰って祀ったところ家運が隆盛。翌年の[[文禄]]元年（[[1592年]]）1月10日、平十郎が神像を拾い上げた場所に社殿を設けた。そののち[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[1681年]]）11月15日、四代目平十郎が創建90年にあたって千代の[[崇福寺 (福岡市)|崇福寺]]境内に社殿を再建する。 [[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]）10月、福岡県の指示により崇福寺境内から[[東公園 (福岡市)|東公園]]に遷座する。[[昭和]]4年（[[1929年]]）には東公園整備に伴って現在の地に遷座する。昭和43年（[[1968年]]）には[[別表神社]]に加わる。 == 施設 == 境内の主要な施設は次の通り。 {{columns-list|3| *[[神社建築|社殿]]（拝殿と本殿の複合社殿） *社務所 *[[手水舎]] *[[鳥居]] *獅子・狛犬 }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|拝殿 File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|本殿 File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine view from Higashi Park 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|[[東公園 (福岡市)|県営東公園]]からの眺め ==十日恵比須正月大祭== 毎年[[1月8日]]から[[1月11日]]まで正月大祭が執り行われる。8日が「初えびす」、9日が「宵えびす」、10日が「正大祭」、11日が「残りえびす」と呼ばれる。正月大祭期間中は福引きや[[芸妓]]かち詣りなどが有名。 ==交通== *[[福岡市地下鉄箱崎線]] [[千代県庁口駅]] *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[鹿児島本線]] [[吉塚駅]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === 出典 === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[神屋宗湛]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ 十日恵比須神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえひすしんしや}} [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:福岡市の神社]] [[Category:博多区の歴史]] [[Category:博多区の建築物]] [[Category:えびす神社]]

{{神社 | 名称=十日恵比須神社 | 画像=[[画像:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | 所在地=福岡県福岡市博多区[[東公園 (福岡市)|東公園]]7-1 | ISO=JP-40 | 緯度度=33|緯度分=36|緯度秒=14 | 経度度=130|経度分=25|経度秒=07 | ラベル位置 = left | 祭神=事代主命
大国主命 | 創建=天正19年（1591年） | 社格=別表神社 | 例祭=[[1月8日]] - [[1月11日|11日]]（十日恵比須祭） | 地図=Fukuoka city }} '''十日恵比須神社'''（とおかえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[博多区]]にある[[神社]]{{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}である。[[事代主|事代主命]]（恵比須神）・[[大国主|大国主命]]（大黒神）を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html 十日恵比寿について]十日恵比寿神社、[[香椎宮]]大宮司家の武内五右衛門（平十郎）は分家して「神屋」と号して博多で商売を営んでいたが、[[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）1月3日、香椎宮・[[筥崎宮]]を参拝したとき、香椎浜に流れついた恵比須神の[[神像]]2体を得た。それを自宅に持ち帰って祀ったところ家運が隆盛。翌年の[[文禄]]元年（[[1592年]]）1月10日、平十郎が神像を拾い上げた場所に社殿を設けた。そののち[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[1681年]]）11月15日、四代目平十郎が創建90年にあたって千代の[[崇福寺 (福岡市)|崇福寺]]境内に社殿を再建する。 [[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]）10月、福岡県の指示により崇福寺境内から[[東公園 (福岡市)|東公園]]に遷座する。[[昭和]]4年（[[1929年]]）には東公園整備に伴って現在の地に遷座する。昭和43年（[[1968年]]）には[[別表神社]]に加わる。 == 施設 == 境内の主要な施設は次の通り。 {{columns-list|3| *[[神社建築|社殿]]（拝殿と本殿の複合社殿） *社務所 *[[手水舎]] *[[鳥居]] *獅子・狛犬 }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|拝殿 File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|本殿 ==十日恵比須正月大祭== 毎年[[1月8日]]から[[1月11日]]まで正月大祭が執り行われる。8日が「初えびす」、9日が「宵えびす」、10日が「正大祭」、11日が「残りえびす」と呼ばれる。正月大祭期間中は福引きや[[芸妓]]かち詣りなどが有名。 ==交通== *[[福岡市地下鉄箱崎線]] [[千代県庁口駅]] *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[鹿児島本線]] [[吉塚駅]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === 出典 === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[神屋宗湛]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ 十日恵比須神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえひすしんしや}} [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:福岡市の神社]] [[Category:博多区の歴史]] [[Category:博多区の建築物]] [[Category:えびす神社]]

revision.comment: /* 歴史 */ 「元和」を「天和」に修正
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=十日恵比須神社 | 画像=[[画像:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | 所在地=福岡県福岡市博多区[[東公園 (福岡市)|東公園]]7-1 | ISO=JP-40 | 緯度度=33|緯度分=36|緯度秒=14 | 経度度=130|経度分=25|経度秒=07 | ラベル位置 = left | 祭神=事代主命
大国主命 | 創建=天正19年（1591年） | 社格=別表神社 | 例祭=[[1月8日]] - [[1月11日|11日]]（十日恵比須祭） | 地図=Fukuoka city }} '''十日恵比須神社'''（とおかえびすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[博多区]]にある[[神社]]{{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}である。[[事代主|事代主命]]（恵比須神）・[[大国主|大国主命]]（大黒神）を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html 十日恵比寿について]十日恵比寿神社、[[香椎宮]]大宮司家の武内五右衛門（平十郎）は分家して「神屋」と号して博多で商売を営んでいたが、[[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）1月3日、香椎宮・[[筥崎宮]]を参拝したとき、香椎浜に流れついた恵比須神の[[神像]]2体を得た。それを自宅に持ち帰って祀ったところ家運が隆盛。翌年の[[文禄]]元年（[[1592年]]）1月10日、平十郎が神像を拾い上げた場所に社殿を設けた。そののち[[天和 (日本)|天和]]元年（[[1681年]]）11月15日、四代目平十郎が創建90年にあたって千代の[[崇福寺 (福岡市)|崇福寺]]境内に社殿を再建する。 [[明治]]12年（[[1879年]]）10月、福岡県の指示により崇福寺境内から[[東公園 (福岡市)|東公園]]に遷座する。[[昭和]]4年（[[1929年]]）には東公園整備に伴って現在の地に遷座する。昭和43年（[[1968年]]）には[[別表神社]]に加わる。 == 施設 == 境内の主要な施設は次の通り。 {{columns-list|3| *[[神社建築|社殿]]（拝殿と本殿の複合社殿） *社務所 *[[手水舎]] *[[鳥居]] *獅子・狛犬 }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|拝殿 File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|本殿 ==十日恵比須正月大祭== 毎年[[1月8日]]から[[1月11日]]まで正月大祭が執り行われる。8日が「初えびす」、9日が「宵えびす」、10日が「正大祭」、11日が「残りえびす」と呼ばれる。正月大祭期間中は福引きや[[芸妓]]かち詣りなどが有名。 ==交通== *[[福岡市地下鉄箱崎線]] [[千代県庁口駅]] *[[九州旅客鉄道]][[鹿児島本線]] [[吉塚駅]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === 出典 === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[神屋宗湛]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ 十日恵比須神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえひすしんしや}} [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:福岡市の神社]] [[Category:博多区の歴史]] [[Category:博多区の建築物]] [[Category:えびす神社]]

{{Shrine | Name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | Image=[[Image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | Location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | ISO=JP-40 | Latitude_deg=33|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=14 | Longitude_deg=130|Longitude_min=25|Longitude_sec=07 | Label_position=left | Deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | Founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | Shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | Annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu Festival) | Map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's Festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main Festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's Festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[Image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|Longitude_min=25|Longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu Festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's Festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main Festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's Festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's Festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main Festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's Festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Latitude_min=36|Latitude_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|Longitude_min=25|Longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu Festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's Festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main Festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's Festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|rank_min=36|rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|Longitude_min=25|Longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]]

{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]]

revision.comment: Bumping all of these pages so that next round does not have any of them in it
fixing links to shrine instead of shinto shrine
Removing from [[Category:Pages that were the oldest for a long time so I am bumping them up the queue for this round]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|rank_min=36|rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:Images]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|rank_min=36|rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|rank_min=36|rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|rank_min=36|rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|rank_min=36|rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]]

During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|rank_min=36|rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokaebisu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|rank_min=36|rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|rank_min=36|rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=[[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|rank_min=36|rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}}.
