{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image= | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{DEFAULTSORT:とおかえびすじんじゃ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tokaebisu Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|十日恵比須神社|version=102019448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Tokaebisu Shrine]] [[ja:十日恵比須神社]] [[en:Tokaebisu Shrine]]

During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|十日恵比須神社|version=102019448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Tokaebisu Shrine]] [[ja:十日恵比須神社]] [[en:Tokaebisu Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image= | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|十日恵比須神社|version=102019448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tokaebisu Shrine]] }} [[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Tokaebisu Shrine]] [[ja:十日恵比須神社]] [[en:Tokaebisu Shrine]]

The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geisha|qq=}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|qq=}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|qq=}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Railway Company|qq=}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshizuka Station|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya Sōtan|qq=}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|十日恵比須神社|version=102019448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Tokaebisu Shrine]] [[ja:十日恵比須神社]] [[en:Tokaebisu Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image= | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank={{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} | annual_festival={{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 8|qq=}} - {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 11|qq=}} (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City|ja|福岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hakata Ward|ja|博多区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata Ward|qq=}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiigu|qq=}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakozaki Shrine|qq=}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1591|qq=}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|qq=}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1592|qq=}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|qq=}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1681|qq=}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1929|qq=}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 8|qq=}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 11|qq=}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geisha|qq=}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|qq=}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|qq=}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Railway Company|qq=}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshizuka Station|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya Sōtan|qq=}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|十日恵比須神社|version=102019448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Tokaebisu Shrine]] [[ja:十日恵比須神社]] [[en:Tokaebisu Shrine]]

It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashiigu|ja|香椎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiigu|qq=}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|ja|筥崎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakozaki Shrine|qq=}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} ({{ill|1591|ja|1591年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1591|qq=}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|qq=}} ({{ill|1592|ja|1592年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1592|qq=}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|qq=}} ({{ill|1681|ja|1681年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1681|qq=}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} ({{ill|1879|ja|1879年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} ({{ill|1929|ja|1929年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1929|qq=}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|ja|1968年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1968|qq=}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=}} *{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|ja|1月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 8|qq=}} to {{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 11|qq=}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|ja|芸妓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geisha|qq=}} processions are famous. == Access == *{{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄箱崎線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|qq=}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|ja|千代県庁口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|qq=}} *{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Railway Company|qq=}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|ja|吉塚駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshizuka Station|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya Sōtan|qq=}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|十日恵比須神社|version=102019448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Tokaebisu Shrine]] [[ja:十日恵比須神社]] [[en:Tokaebisu Shrine]]

In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image= | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank=[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:January 8|January 8]] - [[:en:January 11|January 11]] (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|qq=}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image= | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank=[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:January 8|January 8]] - [[:en:January 11|January 11]] (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city }} '''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|qq=}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous. == Access == *[[:en:Hakozaki Line|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line]] [[:en:Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station]] *[[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]] [[:en:Yoshizuka Station|Yoshizuka Station]] == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya Sōtan|qq=}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|十日恵比須神社|version=102019448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Hakata Ward]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Tokaebisu Shrine]] [[ja:十日恵比須神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Hakata-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:博多区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka]] {{Commons category|Tōka-Ebisu-jinja}}

It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku). == History == According to the shrine's tradition[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|qq=}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|qq=}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding. In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines. == Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall == Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous. == Access == *[[:en:Hakozaki Line|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line]] [[:en:Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station]] *[[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]] [[:en:Yoshizuka Station|Yoshizuka Station]] == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}} == See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya Sōtan|qq=}} == External links == *[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/ Tōka Ebisu Shrine] (official site) {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|十日恵比須神社|version=102019448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ebisu shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[image:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg|250px]] [[sn:Tokaebisu Shrine]] [[ja:十日恵比須神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Hakata-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:博多区の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] {{Commons category|Tōka-Ebisu-jinja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tōka-Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11405077]] Wikidata name: Tōka-ebisu Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=File:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank=[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:January 8|January 8]] - [[:en:January 11|January 11]] (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city | prefecture={{ill|Hakata-ku|qid=Q1330839}}; {{ill|Higashi-kōen|qid=Q11526062}} | coordinates={{Coord|33.60389|N|130.41861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|qq=|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.
== Access == *[[:en:Hakozaki Line|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line]] [[:en:Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station]] *[[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]] [[:en:Yoshizuka Station|Yoshizuka Station]]
== References == {{Reflist}} <references group="lower-alpha" /> === Notes === {{columns-list|2|{{Notelist}}}} === Sources === {{columns-list|2|{{Reflist}}}}
== See also == * {{ill|Kamiya Sōtan|ja|神屋宗湛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya Sōtan|qq=|1=Kamiya Sōtan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589521}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tōka-Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11405077]] Wikidata name: Tōka-ebisu Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=File:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank=[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:January 8|January 8]] - [[:en:January 11|January 11]] (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city | prefecture={{ill|Hakata-ku|qid=Q1330839}}; {{ill|Higashi-kōen|qid=Q11526062}} | coordinates={{Coord|33.60389|N|130.41861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|qq=|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|qq=|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.

'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.

== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tōka-Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11405077]] Wikidata name: Tōka-ebisu Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=File:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank=[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:January 8|January 8]] - [[:en:January 11|January 11]] (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city | prefecture={{ill|Hakata-ku|WD=Q1330839}}; {{ill|Higashi-kōen|WD=Q11526062}} | coordinates={{Coord|33.60389|N|130.41861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tōka-Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11405077]] Wikidata name: Tōka-ebisu Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=File:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank=[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:January 8|January 8]] - [[:en:January 11|January 11]] (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city | prefecture={{ill|Hakata-ku|WD=Q1330839}}; {{ill|Higashi-kōen|WD=Q11526062}} | coordinates={{Coord|33.60389|N|130.41861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.

'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.
== Access == *[[:en:Hakozaki Line|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line]] [[:en:Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station]] *[[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]] [[:en:Yoshizuka Station|Yoshizuka Station]]

'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.

Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.

'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.

== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.
