<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tōka-Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tōka-Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tōka-Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11405077]] Wikidata name: Tōka-ebisu Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=File:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank=[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:January 8|January 8]] - [[:en:January 11|January 11]] (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city | prefecture={{ill|Hakata-ku|WD=Q1330839}}; {{ill|Higashi-kōen|WD=Q11526062}} | coordinates={{Coord|33.60389|N|130.41861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tōka-Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tōka-Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tōka-Ebisu-jinja]][[ja:十日恵比須神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11405077]] Wikidata name: Tōka-ebisu Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tōka Ebisu Shrine | image=File:Tookaebisushrine01.jpg | location=Fukuoka Prefecture, Fukuoka City, Hakata Ward, {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} 7-1 | iSO=JP-40 | latitude_deg=33|Rank_min=36|Rank_sec=14 | longitude_deg=130|longitude_min=25|longitude_sec=07 | label_position=left | deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | founded=19th year of Tenshō (1591) | shrine_Rank=[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | annual_festival=[[:en:January 8|January 8]] - [[:en:January 11|January 11]] (Tōka Ebisu festival) | map=Fukuoka city | prefecture={{ill|Hakata-ku|WD=Q1330839}}; {{ill|Higashi-kōen|WD=Q11526062}} | coordinates={{Coord|33.60389|N|130.41861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.
== Access == *[[:en:Hakozaki Line|Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line]] [[:en:Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station]] *[[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]] [[:en:Yoshizuka Station|Yoshizuka Station]]

'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata Ward]] {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).
== History == According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting [[:en:Kashii-gū|Kashiigu]] and [[:en:Hakozaki Shrine|Hakozaki Shrine]] on January 3, 19th year of [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] ([[:en:1591|1591]]). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] ([[:en:1592|1592]]), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ([[:en:1681|1681]]), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.
In October of the 12th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] ([[:en:1879|1879]]), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] ([[:en:1929|1929]]), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ([[:en:1968|1968]]), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.
== Facilities == The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows: {{columns-list|3| *[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall) *Shrine office *{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}} *[[:en:Torii|Torii]] *Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu) }} <gallery> File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall </gallery>
== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival == Every year, a grand festival is held from [[:en:January 8|January 8]] to [[:en:January 11|January 11]]. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and [[:en:Geisha|Geisha]] processions are famous.

'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City}}, {{ill|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|en|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1330839|lt=Hakata Ward}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).

== History ==
According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashii-gū|en|Kashii-gū|WD=Q631373|lt=Kashiigu}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|en|Hakozaki Shrine|WD=Q714742|lt=Hakozaki Shrine}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku}} ({{ill|1592|en|1592|WD=Q6769|lt=1592}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ({{ill|1681|en|1681|WD=Q7645|lt=1681}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.

In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} ({{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.

== Facilities ==
The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows:

*Shrine office

}}

== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival ==
Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|en|January 8|WD=Q2234|lt=January 8}} to {{ill|January 11|en|January 11|WD=Q2247|lt=January 11}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|en|Geisha|WD=Q82723|lt=Geisha}} processions are famous.

'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City}}, {{ill|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|en|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1330839|lt=Hakata Ward}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).

== History ==
According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashii-gū|en|Kashii-gū|WD=Q631373|lt=Kashiigu}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|en|Hakozaki Shrine|WD=Q714742|lt=Hakozaki Shrine}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku}} ({{ill|1592|en|1592|WD=Q6769|lt=1592}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ({{ill|1681|en|1681|WD=Q7645|lt=1681}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.

In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} ({{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.

== Facilities ==
The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows:
{{columns-list|3|
*[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall)
*Shrine office
*{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}}
*{{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}}
*Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu)
}}
<gallery>
File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall
File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall
</gallery>

== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival ==
Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|en|January 8|WD=Q2234|lt=January 8}} to {{ill|January 11|en|January 11|WD=Q2247|lt=January 11}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|en|Geisha|WD=Q82723|lt=Geisha}} processions are famous.

== Access ==
*{{ill|Hakozaki Line|en|Hakozaki Line|WD=Q1073881|lt=Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|en|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|WD=Q4357546|lt=Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station}}
*{{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}} {{ill|Yoshizuka Station|en|Yoshizuka Station|WD=Q872292|lt=Yoshizuka Station}}

The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|en|Geisha|WD=Q82723|lt=Geisha}} processions are famous.

== Access ==
*{{ill|Hakozaki Line|en|Hakozaki Line|WD=Q1073881|lt=Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line}} {{ill|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|en|Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station|WD=Q4357546|lt=Chiyo-Kenchōguchi Station}}

'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City|1=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|en|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1330839|lt=Hakata Ward|1=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).

== History ==
According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashii-gū|en|Kashii-gū|WD=Q631373|lt=Kashiigu|1=Kashii-gū}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|en|Hakozaki Shrine|WD=Q714742|lt=Hakozaki Shrine|1=Hakozaki Shrine}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591|1=1591}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} ({{ill|1592|en|1592|WD=Q6769|lt=1592|1=1592}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ({{ill|1681|en|1681|WD=Q7645|lt=1681|1=1681}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.

In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879|1=1879}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Q11526059|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.

== Facilities ==
The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows:
{{columns-list|3|
*[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall)
*Shrine office
*{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=Chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}}
*{{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}}
*Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu)
}}

</gallery>

The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|en|Geisha|WD=Q82723|lt=Geisha|1=Geisha}} processions are famous.

According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashii-gū|en|Kashii-gū|WD=Q631373|lt=Kashiigu|1=Kashii-gū}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|en|Hakozaki Shrine|WD=Q714742|lt=Hakozaki Shrine|1=Hakozaki Shrine}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591|1=1591}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} ({{ill|1592|en|1592|WD=Q6769|lt=1592|1=1592}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ({{ill|1681|en|1681|WD=Q7645|lt=1681|1=1681}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.

In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879|1=1879}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Q11526059|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.

== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival ==
Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|en|January 8|WD=Q2234|lt=January 8|1=January 8}} to {{ill|January 11|en|January 11|WD=Q2247|lt=January 11|1=January 11}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|en|Geisha|WD=Q82723|lt=Geisha|1=Geisha}} processions are famous.

'''Tōka Ebisu Shrine''' (十日恵比須神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City|1=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|en|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1330839|lt=Hakata Ward|1=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka}} {{Efn|法人番号：2290005000477<ref name = "houjin-bangou">{{Cite web|和書| author = 国税庁長官官房企画課法人番号管理室 | url = https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/ | title = 国税庁法人番号公表サイト | accessdate = 2022-10-30 }}→検索</ref>}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (Ebisu) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Daikoku).

== History ==
According to the shrine's tradition<ref>[http://www.tooka-ebisu.or.jp/history.html About Tōka Ebisu] Tōka Ebisu Shrine</ref>, Takeuchi Goemon (Heijiro) of the Kashiigu Ōmiya clan, who operated a business under the name "Kamiya" in Hakata, found two divine images of Ebisu that had washed ashore at Kashiihama while visiting {{ill|Kashii-gū|en|Kashii-gū|WD=Q631373|lt=Kashiigu|1=Kashii-gū}} and {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|en|Hakozaki Shrine|WD=Q714742|lt=Hakozaki Shrine|1=Hakozaki Shrine}} on January 3, 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|1591|en|1591|WD=Q6766|lt=1591|1=1591}}). He brought them home and enshrined them, leading to his family's prosperity. The following year, on January 10, 1st year of {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} ({{ill|1592|en|1592|WD=Q6769|lt=1592|1=1592}}), Heijiro built a shrine at the place where he found the divine images. Later, on November 15, 1st year of {{ill|Tenna (Japan)|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna (Japan)|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} ({{ill|1681|en|1681|WD=Q7645|lt=1681|1=1681}}), the fourth-generation Heijiro rebuilt the shrine within the grounds of Sōfukuji Temple in Chiyo to commemorate the 90th anniversary of its founding.

In October of the 12th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879|1=1879}}), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|ja|東公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Park (Fukuoka)|1=Q11526059|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526059}} by order of Fukuoka Prefecture. In the 4th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} ({{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}}), it was moved to its current location due to the redevelopment of Higashi Park. In the 43rd year of Shōwa ({{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}), it was included in the list of Beppyo shrines.

== Facilities ==
The main facilities within the shrine grounds are as follows:
{{columns-list|3|
*[[Shrine architecture|Shrine building]] (a combined worship and main hall)
*Shrine office
*{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=Chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1069667}}
*{{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}}
*Lion-dogs (Shishi and Komainu)
}}
<gallery>
File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine haiden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Worship hall
File:Tōka-ebisu Jinja shintō shrine honden 7-1 Higashikōen Hakata-ku Fukuoka City 20221115.jpg|Main hall
</gallery>

== Tōka Ebisu New Year's festival ==
Every year, a grand festival is held from {{ill|January 8|en|January 8|WD=Q2234|lt=January 8|1=January 8}} to {{ill|January 11|en|January 11|WD=Q2247|lt=January 11|1=January 11}}. The 8th is called "Hatsu Ebisu," the 9th is "Yoi Ebisu," the 10th is the "Main festival," and the 11th is "Nokori Ebisu." During the New Year's festival, events like lotteries and {{ill|Geisha|en|Geisha|WD=Q82723|lt=Geisha|1=Geisha}} processions are famous.
